text:
Market today: Mood is cautious-to-mixed. The big under-the-hood story is headline 8 - quant/momentum funds just had their worst rout of the year. That's a direct headwind for high-momentum tech and chip names, exactly what fills your book. Nothing in today's news is a clean bullish catalyst for your sector.

Your holdings: SOXL (-7.06%) is the one demanding attention - it's a 3x leveraged chip ETF already down hard, and your stop is at $200 vs current $236. On margin, a leveraged position going wrong compounds fast. SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with almost no price history - tiny moves can be wild; margin amplifies that pain. SMH (-0.69%) is minor but watch it given the momentum quant selloff hitting semis directly.

Watch: (1) Whether SMH holds above $600 - that's the canary for your whole chip exposure. (2) SOXL's behavior near $200 stop - do not let that slide.

Bottom line: Protect the leveraged and IPO positions first; today is a day to guard capital, not add risk.
plays: SOXL: Your stop is $200 - honor it, no exceptions. On margin with 3x leverage, a momentum-driven selloff can gap through stops; consider tightening toward $215-220 if you want less exposure.
SPCX: Keep position small. Brand-new IPO, thin data, margin underneath - if it breaks $150 stop, exit without hesitation. No reason to add here.
No new buys today - momentum quant rout is still fresh and your chip/tech book is already heavily exposed. Hold, watch stops, let the dust settle.
